summary_one_line: Reaching xmlNewNs's false branch requires a well-formed XML doc with a non-xml namespace declaration parsed through the SAX2 attribute path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes that satisfy the multi-byte string equality gates on the path to xmlNewNs — the 'xmlns'/'xml:' attribute-name comparisons (att[0]=='x' && att[1]=='m' && att[2]=='l' && att[3]=='n' && att[4]=='s') and xmlStrEqual checks — so the parser issues a namespace declaration and calls xmlNewNs, then takes the false branch because prefix != 'xml'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winners drive each character-by-character CMP closer to matching via the CMP_MAP distance buckets, so partially-correct namespace tokens are retained and refined until the full 'xmlns' literal is assembled; their branch hitcount climbs to 89 (vp>naive) and 15 (vpc>cmplog) versus the loser's ~1-4, showing repeated successful calls into xmlNewNs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog get no per-CMP closeness gradient: a partly-correct 'xmlns' token earns no new coverage bucket, so the multi-byte equality is only solved by blind luck — losers stay blocked in 8/10 trials with branch hitcount of 1-4.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each intercepted integer/string CMP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry, and an input closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, giving a hill-climbing signal that solves the xmlns/xmlStrEqual roadblocks that naive/cmplog must brute-force.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed nested XML document (DOCTYPE + nested elements with namespace-bearing attributes) so the SAX2 start-element path repeatedly invokes xmlNewNs for valid non-xml prefixes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's generalization + structural splicing recombine generalised token/gap structures into deeply valid XML; its winning seeds show GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator provenance and a branch hitcount of 1959 (vs loser 1), i.e. it manufactures hundreds of valid namespace declarations per execution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) has I2S but no structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the nested well-formed element structure that produces many xmlNewNs calls — it stays blocked in 8/10 trials with hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on those generalised structures, building grammar-valid nested XML that the cmplog baseline (same I2S, no generalization) cannot construct.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML input that reaches xmlNewNs through the SAX2 attribute-internal call context (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal -> xmlNewNs at SAX2.c:1214), where the call path itself is the distinguishing signal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the xmlNewNs edge via the SAX2 namespace-declaration call context counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage; this rewards inputs progressing down the attribute path and lifts branch hitcount to 79 with 8/10 resolved.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive records only the bare edge, so once any path has touched the xmlNewNs region it earns no further credit for reaching it through the namespace-declaration context, leaving the path under-explored — naive resolves only 2/10 with hitcount 4.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: context-sensitive coverage: naive_ctx's CtxHook keeps a running hash of the caller chain and indexes the edge map by (call-context, edge), so the SAX2-attribute call path into xmlNewNs is treated as fresh coverage and explored, whereas naive collapses it to one already-seen edge.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the prefix=='xml' test in xmlNewNs; the winners take the false branch.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
xmlNewNs is reached through xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at SAX2.c:1214 on a winner-only path.
The path to xmlNewNs passes through multi-byte 'xmlns' character-equality gates.
value_profile (pair 3) raises branch hitcount to 89 vs naive's 4 while resolving 9/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=89  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog (pair 1) resolves 10/10 and raises hitcount to 15 vs cmplog's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=15  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand closeness and preserves inputs that get closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire (pair 2) drives branch hitcount to 1959 vs cmplog's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1959  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire winning seeds carry Grimoire structural-mutator provenance.
grimoire's single-technique delta over cmplog is generalization plus Grimoire structural mutators (both have I2S).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice grammar-free structural mutations over generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx (pair 4) resolves 8/10 vs naive 2/10 and raises hitcount to 79 vs 4.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=79  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds call-context into the edge-map index so the same edge is new coverage per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: For naive_ctx a new bucket is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses only naive's havoc/token mutators with no I2S substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Primary hypothesis is value_profile CMP_MAP gradient, for which lineage I2S-floor verification does not apply (vp has no I2SRandReplace stage). For corroboration I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6924 --role W --fuzzer grimoire --trial 1 --seed 8f8537181bfef3b5` and saw I2SRandReplace ops plus 1 dash row at depth 4, but per the 10-fuzzer caveat dash rows are NOT I2S-exclusive against grimoire, so this is not treated as a CMP_MAP verification.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating xmlNewNs behind the 'xmlns' string-equality CMPs in which value_profile fails to resolve the false branch faster than naive/cmplog (no resolution-rate gain from the CMP_MAP distance gradient).

weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L byte divergence, so the specific input bytes that distinguish the three winning techniques are inferred from source/hitcount rather than directly diffed.
